summary: Spectacular matrix recording captures a high‑energy 1981 Garden show with an explosive first set and standout performances of Ramble On Rose, The Other One and numerous classics.
review: The show is hailed as one of the strongest early‑80s Dead outings, with a monster first set that rolls nonstop into a second half packed with tight jams and inspired solos. The Hunter matrix mix is described as crystal‑clear, perfectly balanced and immersive, making every instrument—from Jerry’s razor‑sharp lead to Brent’s mind‑blowing keyboard solo—shine.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviewers give 4–5 stars and praise both the musical performance and the matrix recording, indicating an exceptional show with excellent sound quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with balanced instrument levels; audience ambience is present but well‑controlled, giving a live‑album feel. Vocals sit nicely without harshness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as a quintessential, high‑energy 80s opener that never lets up; second set maintains momentum with strong jams and seamless transitions, exemplifying the flow of a classic NYC Garden concert.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Ripping lead guitar throughout, tone crisp, clear and front‑center; likened to Robert Fripp in space.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals in excellent form; his rhythmic ‘tic‑tac’ patterns praised.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland well represented, standout solo on “Minglewood”, overall keyboard work highlighted as a highlight of the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Rhythm Devils hold down the groove; drums described as musical and very listenable.
